DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE DEC-08-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO THE CITY'S INTERROGATORIES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS MOOT. PLAINTIFF SHALL WITHIN TEN DAYS OF THE DATE OF THE ORDER TO BE ENTERED PROVIDE A VERIFICATION TO THE DISCOVERY RESPONSES SERVED AFTER THE FILING OF THE MOTION. IT APPEARS THE CONDITION OF A TRIAL CONTINUANCE BEING GRANTED WILL RESULT IN THE MOTION BEING DROPPED WILL HAPPEN AS THE CONTINUANCE MOTION BEING HEARD IN DEPT. 206 TODAY IS UNOPPOSED. COURT TO SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY DEFENSE COUNSEL. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVE STEIN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 18, 2022. PLAINTIFF DEMONSTRATES GOOD CAUSE FOR A CONTINUANCE PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(H). (SEE PATTEN DECL.) PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION IS DUE ON MARCH 7, 2022 AND DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY IS DUE ON MARCH 14, 2022. PLATINIFF IS REMINDED TO DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ALL PLEADINGS TO DEPARTMENT 302 PER LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). THE COURT WILL ADDRESS ALL OF THE ISSUES RAISED IN THIS MOTION, INCLUDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF EXHAUSTED HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES ON THE RETALIATION CLAIM, AFTER THE MARCH 18, 2022 HEARING. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS, CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/SRB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Continued To Mar-18-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication

The motion for summary judgment is continued to March 18, 2022. Plaintiff demonstrates good cause for a continuance pursuant to CCP 437c(h). (See Patten Decl.) Plaintiff's supplemental opposition is due on March 7, 2022 and defendant's supplemental reply is due on March 14, 2022. Platiniff is reminded to deliver courtesy copies of all pleadings to Department 302 per Local Rule 2.7(B). The court will address all of the issues raised in this motion, including whether plaintiff exhausted her administrative remedies on the retaliation claim, after the March 18, 2022 hearing. Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)

Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DOW W. PATTEN, Esq. of Smith Patten (Tel: 415-402-0084) for plaintiff Lisa Wy;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To The City's Interrogatories.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and plaintiff's counsel both authorize the clerk to sign the stipulate form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion to compel granted in part and denied in part as moot. Plaintiff shall within ten days of the date of the order to be entered provide a verification to the discovery responses served after the filing of the motion. It appears the condition of a trial continuance being granted will result in the motion being dropped will happen as the continuance motion being heard in Dept. 206 today is unopposed.

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by defense counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
